name: Viper (Go)
what_it_does: Complete configuration solution for Go applications. Supports reading from config files (JSON, TOML, YAML, HCL, INI, dotenv), environment variables, remote config systems (etcd, Consul), and command-line flags. Integrates with Cobra for CLI flag binding. Handles 12-factor app configuration patterns with priority ordering (flags > env > config > defaults).
use_cases: Manage application configuration from multiple sources (files, env vars, flags) with a single priority-ordered API
Bind Cobra CLI flags to Viper config values for consistent configuration across CLI and server modes
Load configuration from remote sources (etcd, Consul) with live config reload for long-running services
Use environment variable overrides for Docker/Kubernetes deployments without changing config files
Provide typed configuration access (GetString, GetInt, GetDuration) with sensible defaults
not_for: Strongly-typed config structs at compile time — use envconfig or cleanenv for struct-based config validation
Secrets management — use HashiCorp Vault or cloud KMS; Viper handles config, not secrets
best_when: You're building a Go application or CLI that needs layered configuration from files, environment variables, and flags with a clean priority hierarchy.
avoid_when: You need compile-time config validation or strongly-typed structs — consider alternatives with struct tags for type safety.

auth.notes: Library with no auth requirement. Remote config backends (etcd, Consul) require their own auth configuration.

pricing.notes: Free and open source. Maintained by Steve Francia (spf13), part of the Cobra/Viper CLI toolkit.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Viper returns zero-values for missing keys rather than errors — agents must use IsSet() to distinguish missing vs zero-value config. Config file parse errors return Go errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Config reads are idempotent. Viper's global state can cause issues in tests — use viper.New() for isolated instances.
known_agent_gotchas: Viper uses a global singleton by default — concurrent tests that modify Viper config will conflict; always use viper.New() for test isolation
GetString() returns empty string for missing keys, not an error — use IsSet() or required config validation to catch missing required fields
Environment variable binding requires explicit BindEnv() or AutomaticEnv() call — env vars are NOT automatically read without enabling AutomaticEnv
AutomaticEnv with SetEnvKeyReplacer replaces dots with underscores by default — config key 'server.port' maps to env var 'SERVER_PORT'
Viper config keys are case-insensitive — 'Database.Host' and 'database.host' are the same key, which can cause confusion with YAML/JSON keys
WatchConfig() for live reload uses fsnotify and only works on local files — remote config (etcd/Consul) has different reload mechanisms

security_score_components.security_notes: Config files may contain secrets — Viper provides no encryption. Use separate secrets management. AutomaticEnv is convenient but can accidentally expose config via env vars if keys conflict.
